mechanism_narrative: Positive trailing growth in retail sales indicates strengthening household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, so higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 389320 as of one month before 2011-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS growth signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window or positive-signal instances do not exceed baseline 20-day returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment crosses below its 3-month moving average while still elevated, improving labor-market slack raises income expectations and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below its 3-month moving average while UNRATE is above 8.0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 8.8 as of one month before 2011-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in 50% or fewer cases, or if their mean return is not above non-triggered periods at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts collapse below recessionary thresholds signals weak construction demand, impaired household formation, and credit stress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 600 thousand annualized units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 479 as of two months before 2011-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances generate positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below baseline at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing into the zero-bound region lowers cash yields and discount rates, pushing investors toward equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.14 as of one month before 2011-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS below 0.25% is followed by below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if Rank IC is negative over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, real income pressure and policy-tightening fears dominate liquidity support, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 4%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 223.49 and FEDFUNDS 0.14 as of one month before 2011-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the interaction coefficient is not negative at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in industrial production and payrolls confirms that output and employment are reinforcing each other, raising earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both post positive 3-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 93.5904 and PAYEMS 130738 as of one month before 2011-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive-growth triggers do not outperform periods with only one positive series over 60 trading days, or if the interaction term is not positive at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A payroll-employment growth break below zero signals renewed labor-market contraction and lower aggregate income, causing earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 130738 as of one month before 2011-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS negative-change triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns exceed baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth signals broad final-demand expansion and improving corporate cash flows, so higher GDPC1 growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value 13380.7 as of six months before 2011-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth has Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 5-year windows or if positive-GDPC1-growth quarters fail to produce above-baseline 60-day returns at p < 0.10.
